operations.name: index
operations.summary: Parse a repo (or working tree) with tree-sitter, extract symbols/imports/call-edges/doc-comments, and persist a graph + symbol index + lexical (BM25/trigram) index. Full index on first run; incremental git-diff delta against the prior snapshot thereafter. The single ingest entrypoint every other op reads from. Ports CodeIntelligenceService.IndexRepository.
operations.inputs: path|repo (local dir path OR hosted repo ref org/name); ref/commit (optional, defaults to working tree / default branch HEAD); base (optional commit for explicit delta); langs[] (optional filter); reindex (bool, force full vs delta); enable_vectors (bool, default false — opt-in embedding pass behind VectorStore)
operations.outputs: { repo_id, snapshot_id, commit_sha, indexed_files, symbols, edges, routes, delta:{added,modified,removed}, duration_ms, mode: full|delta }

operations.name: search
operations.summary: Code-aware lexical search over the symbol/file index: BM25 (bleve) + trigram, with camelCase/snake_case tokenization so identifiers behave semantically. The default retrieval path for agents — no vectors required. Optional semantic re-rank only when vectors are explicitly enabled. Ports CodeSearchService BM25 + code_search_keyword/semantic.
operations.inputs: query (string); repo_id (optional; defaults to current/all linked); kind (optional symbol-kind filter: function|method|class|interface|type|const); path_glob (optional); limit (default 20); mode (lexical|semantic|hybrid, default lexical — semantic/hybrid require enable_vectors)
operations.outputs: { results:[{symbol, kind, repo_id, path, line, signature, doc, score, snippet}], mode_used }

operations.name: symbol
operations.summary: Full context bundle for one symbol: definition (file:line, signature, doc/rationale comment), direct callers, callees/dependencies, and covering tests. The agent's 'open this thing fully' primitive. Ports code_symbol_lookup.
operations.inputs: symbol (exact name) OR symbol_id; repo_id (optional); include_tests (bool, default true); include_source (bool, default false)
operations.outputs: { symbol, kind, repo_id, path, line, signature, doc, callers[], callees[], dependencies[], tests[], source? }

operations.name: callers
operations.summary: Who calls / invokes this symbol — inbound call edges across the graph, transitively up to a depth. Ports CodeSearchService.GetCallers / code_callers_analysis.
operations.inputs: symbol (exact) OR symbol_id; repo_id (optional); depth (default 1); limit (default 100)
operations.outputs: { callers:[{symbol, repo_id, path, line, depth, edge_kind}] }

operations.name: refs
operations.summary: All references/usages of a symbol (reads, writes, type uses, not just calls) across the indexed corpus. Ports CodeSearchService.GetReferences / code_references_analysis.
operations.inputs: symbol (exact) OR symbol_id; repo_id (optional); ref_kind (optional: call|read|write|type|import); limit (default 200)
operations.outputs: { references:[{symbol, repo_id, path, line, ref_kind, snippet}] }

operations.name: neighbors
operations.summary: Adjacent nodes of a graph node (symbol or file) along chosen edge types and direction — the local-subgraph primitive for agent graph-walking. Mirrors graphify get_neighbors but multi-repo aware.
operations.inputs: node (symbol_id|file path|symbol name); repo_id (optional); direction (in|out|both, default both); edge_kinds[] (optional: calls|imports|references|covers|route); depth (default 1); limit (default 100)
operations.outputs: { node, neighbors:[{node, kind, repo_id, edge_kind, direction, depth}] }

operations.name: path
operations.summary: Shortest dependency/call path between two graph nodes (e.g. how does the HTTP handler reach the DB write). Cross-repo capable via route-contract bridge edges. Mirrors graphify shortest_path.
operations.inputs: from (node id|symbol|file); to (node id|symbol|file); edge_kinds[] (optional filter); max_hops (default 8); allow_cross_repo (bool, default true on hosted)
operations.outputs: { found, hops, path:[{node, repo_id, edge_kind}] }

operations.name: impact
operations.summary: Single-repo blast radius for a set of changed symbols or files: transitively impacted symbols, files, and covering tests, ranked by directness. The CI/PR gate's core within-repo primitive. Ports CodeSearchService.GetImpactForChangedSymbols / code_impact_analysis.
operations.inputs: symbols[] OR changed_paths[] OR diff (unified diff); repo_id (optional); max_depth (default 3); include_tests (bool, default true)
operations.outputs: { impacted_symbols[], impacted_files[], impacted_tests[], directness_ranked[], depth_reached }

operations.name: explain
operations.summary: Assemble a grounded, citation-backed context bundle for a symbol/file/change — definition + neighbors + rationale comments + covering tests + recent history — pre-formatted for an LLM to reason over. The 'give me everything an agent needs, cited' op. Reuses Pulse BuildReviewContext-style assembly.
operations.inputs: target (symbol|file|node id) OR changed_paths[]; repo_id (optional); depth (default 2); include_history (bool, default false); budget_tokens (optional cap)
operations.outputs: { summary, citations:[{repo_id, path, line, snippet, kind}], neighbors[], tests[], rationale_notes[], history? }

operations.name: graph_export
operations.summary: Export the knowledge graph (or a subgraph) as a portable graph.json — graphify-compatible schema plus Atlas extensions (repo_id, snapshot_id, route edges, coverage edges). The interop/offline-analysis op. Mirrors graphify graph.json.
operations.inputs: repo_id (optional; all linked if omitted); snapshot_id (optional, defaults latest); scope (full|subgraph); root (node id, required if subgraph); depth (subgraph only); format (graphjson|jsonl); include_source (bool, default false)
operations.outputs: { nodes:[...], edges:[...], meta:{repo_id, snapshot_id, commit_sha, schema_version} } (streamed file/blob)

operations.name: cross_repo_impact
operations.summary: Org-wide blast radius: from a change in one repo, find impacted symbols/tests in OTHER repos by matching HTTP route contracts (producer route -> consumer call site) and shared interfaces. The flagship moat graphify structurally lacks. Ports CodeIntelligenceService.CrossRepoImpactForChangedPaths.
operations.inputs: repo_id; changed_paths[] OR symbols[] OR diff; max_depth (default 2); include_tests (bool, default true)
operations.outputs: { origin_repo, impacted:[{repo_id, symbol, path, line, via: route_contract|interface|import, contract:{method,path}, confidence}], impacted_tests_by_repo{} }

operations.name: consumers
operations.summary: Reverse cross-repo lookup: which repos/services CONSUME a given producer endpoint, interface, or symbol (who depends on me). Powers 'safe to change this API?' Built on the cross-service route-contract analyzer.
operations.inputs: repo_id; target (route {method,path} | symbol | interface); direction (consumers|producers, default consumers)
operations.outputs: { target, matches:[{repo_id, service, path, line, call_kind, contract:{method,path}, confidence}] }

operations.name: route_contracts
operations.summary: List the resolved HTTP route contracts for a repo/org: producer routes (handlers) and consumer call sites, with their match state. The introspection op behind cross_repo_impact/consumers. Ports route_contract_analyzer + cross_service_analyzer output.
operations.inputs: repo_id (optional; org-wide if omitted); role (producer|consumer|all, default all); method (optional); path_glob (optional); matched_only (bool, default false)
operations.outputs: { contracts:[{role, method, path, repo_id, symbol, file, line, matched_to[], confidence}] }

operations.name: history
operations.summary: Temporal history for a symbol/file/repo: the chain of per-commit snapshots that touched it — when it changed, in which commit, and the delta. graphify is point-in-time; Atlas is time-aware. Built on per-commit RepoSnapshot + delta indexing.
operations.inputs: target (symbol|file|repo_id); repo_id (optional); since (commit|date, optional); until (optional); limit (default 50)
operations.outputs: { target, entries:[{snapshot_id, commit_sha, authored_at, change:added|modified|removed, delta_summary}] }

operations.name: snapshot_diff
operations.summary: Structural diff between two snapshots (commits) of a repo: symbols/edges/routes added, modified, removed — graph-level, not text-level. Powers 'what really changed semantically between v1 and v2'. Built on snapshot + delta machinery.
operations.inputs: repo_id; from (snapshot_id|commit); to (snapshot_id|commit, default latest); scope (symbols|edges|routes|all, default all)
operations.outputs: { from, to, added:{symbols[],edges[],routes[]}, modified:{...}, removed:{...}, counts{} }

operations.name: tests_for_change
operations.summary: Predictive test selection: given a change (paths/symbols/diff), return the ranked minimal set of tests to run, derived from symbol->test coverage edges + impact graph + cross-repo reach. The CI cost/safety lever and PR blast-radius gate. Atlas-only vs graphify (test-blind).
operations.inputs: repo_id; changed_paths[] OR symbols[] OR diff; coverage_types[] (ut|e2e|integration, default all); cross_repo (bool, hosted, default true); budget (optional max tests); min_confidence (optional)
operations.outputs: { selected_tests:[{test_id, name, path, repo_id, coverage_type, reason, confidence, directness}], recall_estimate, total_candidates, skipped }

operations.name: coverage
operations.summary: The symbol<->test coverage map: which tests cover a given symbol/file, or which symbols a given test exercises. The substrate tests_for_change is built on; also answers coverage-gap queries. Uses CodeSearchService coverage edges (coverage_type ut|e2e|integration).
operations.inputs: target (symbol|file|test_id); repo_id (optional); direction (tests_for_symbol|symbols_for_test, default tests_for_symbol); coverage_types[] (optional)
operations.outputs: { target, coverage:[{test_id|symbol, path, line, coverage_type, strength}], covered (bool), gap (bool) }

operations.name: rca
operations.summary: Graph-driven root-cause analysis: given a failing test/incident/symptom, walk the graph in REVERSE (symptom -> candidate cause) using impact + cross-repo reach + history to produce a cited, ranked causal hypothesis set. Atlas ACTS — graphify is read-only. Ports SessionRunner.StartRCA wired to the impact engine.
operations.inputs: symptom (failing test id | stack trace | error | changed_paths); repo_id; commit/ref (optional); depth (default 4); include_cross_repo (bool, default true); async (bool, hosted job)
operations.outputs: { run_id, hypotheses:[{candidate_symbol, repo_id, path, line, causal_path[], confidence, citations[], ruled_out:bool}], summary }

operations.name: fix
operations.summary: Graph-grounded autonomous fix: take an RCA hypothesis or a finding, generate a patch with the impacted graph + covering tests as context, validate against tests_for_change, and open/draft a PR. The action surface. Ports SessionRunner.StartFix / StartFixFromPRReviewFinding.
operations.inputs: target (rca run_id | finding_id | symbol + intent); repo_id; mode (draft_pr|comment|patch_only, default draft_pr); validate (bool, run selected tests, default true); async (bool)
operations.outputs: { run_id, status, patch|diff, pr_url?, files_changed[], tests_run[], validation:{passed,failed} }

operations.name: review
operations.summary: Graph-aware PR review hook: for a PR/diff, compute single- and cross-repo impact + selected tests, assemble cited review context, and emit (and optionally post) findings + a merge-gate verdict. The CI/webhook entrypoint. Ports BuildReviewContext + impact + StartFixFromPRReview wiring.
operations.inputs: repo_id; pr_number OR diff/base..head; post (bool, post to GitHub, default false); gate (bool, return pass/block merge verdict, default true); cross_repo (bool, default true)
operations.outputs: { run_id, findings:[{path,line,severity,what_changed,risk,citation}], impact_summary, cross_repo_impact, selected_tests[], verdict:pass|block, posted:bool }

operations.name: status
operations.summary: Health + indexing state of the engine: store driver in use, vector backend (if any), per-repo index freshness, queue depth, last snapshot per repo. The 'is it ready / what's stale' op for CLI, daemon, and monitoring.
operations.inputs: repo_id (optional; engine-wide if omitted); verbose (bool)
operations.outputs: { tier, storage_driver, vector_backend|null, repos_indexed, queue:{pending,running,failed}, repos:[{repo_id, last_snapshot, commit_sha, indexed_at, stale}] }

operations.name: repos
operations.summary: List the repositories known to the engine and their index/snapshot metadata. Local tier: the folders indexed on this machine; hosted tier: the org's linked repos. Ports RepoIndex/RepoIndexJob listing.
operations.inputs: filter (optional name glob); include_stats (bool, default true)
operations.outputs: { repos:[{repo_id, name, path|remote, default_branch, snapshots, symbols, last_indexed, language_breakdown}] }

operations.name: link
operations.summary: Register/link a repository (and its git webhook) into the org-wide hosted graph so it participates in cross-repo impact, durable indexing queue, and webhook-driven delta reindexing. The hosted-tier onboarding op; local tier resolves this to a no-op/path-add. Backed by RepoIndexJob + webhook wiring.
operations.inputs: repo (remote url|org/name OR local path); default_branch (optional); webhook (bool, default true on hosted); index_now (bool, default true)
operations.outputs: { repo_id, linked:bool, webhook_registered:bool, initial_job_id, status }
